evidence: Supabase instrumentation mutates global runtime surfaces (`console.warn`, `window.WebSocket`, `window.onerror`, `window.onunhandledrejection`) in `src/integrations/supabase/instrumentation/realtime/index.ts` and `src/integrations/supabase/instrumentation/window/index.ts`.

`src/integrations/supabase/runtime/supabaseRuntime.ts` installs instrumentation during client runtime init, so cross-module behavior depends on init order and side effects rather than explicit contracts.

Mixed style islands in assigned files: `src/app/routes.tsx` uses both single and double quotes in adjacent imports and contains stale explanatory comments, while nearby app modules use cleaner, consistent formatting.
Naming drift persists in gallery controller: implementation name `useGenerationsPageLogic` is exported as `useGalleryPageState` in `src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts`.

`src/features/billing/hooks/useCredits.ts` constructs and throws new `Error(...)` strings for fetch failures, while `src/features/account/hooks/useApiTokens.ts` and `src/features/settings/hooks/useApiKeys.ts` throw raw Supabase errors.

`src/features/ai/hooks/useAIInteractionService.ts` exposes `*Safe` variants that swallow errors and return defaults, whereas `src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts` catches and returns booleans, producing inconsistent caller contracts.
`src/features/ai/hooks/useAIInteractionService.ts` duplicates each operation across `OrThrow`, regular, and `Safe` wrappers, adding layered indirection with only small behavioral differences.

`src/domains/generation/hooks/useSmartPolling.ts` and `src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx` are pass-through wrappers/re-exports that preserve old placement instead of adding domain logic.

Public hook APIs in the same area expose divergent failure contracts: exception-throwing (`useCredits` internals), nullable fallback (`generateSummarySafe`), and boolean status returns (`handleAddToShot`).
Gallery module keeps two public names for one behavior (`useGenerationsPageLogic` + `useGalleryPageState` alias), increasing API ambiguity.

Most feature hooks enforce auth through `requireSession`/`requireUserFromSession` (`useCredits`, `useApiTokens`, `useApiKeys`), but `src/app/hooks/useAuthGuard.ts` and `src/app/hooks/useOnboardingFlow.ts` directly use `supabase.auth.getSession()`/table reads.
Route-level gating in `src/app/Layout.tsx` is strong, but data-access hooks still use mixed explicit-auth patterns.
`src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts` includes large descriptive banners and repeated section separators that outsize functional signal.
`src/integrations/supabase/reconnect/ReconnectScheduler.ts` has placeholder comments such as 'can be added later if needed' and repetitive explanatory scaffolding around straightforward logic.
Deprecated compatibility export remains active in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` (`supabase` proxy with `@deprecated` and removal target `2026-06-30`).

`src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx` is explicitly a migration shim re-export, and `src/domains/generation/hooks/useSmartPolling.ts` forwards to shared hook instead of native domain ownership.

Objective structure signals: root config wrappers are peripheral stragglers (`tailwind.config.ts`, `vite.config.ts`, `vitest.config.ts`, `vitest.edge.config.ts` fan_in < 5 in `holistic_context.structure.root_files`) while canonical logic is already under `config/*`.

`src/domains/generation/hooks/useSmartPolling.ts` and `src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx` are import-affinity mismatches: files live in domain/feature packages but forward directly to `src/shared/*`, indicating unfinished moves and blurred ownership.

Top-level app/runtime split is clear (`src/app/bootstrap.tsx`, `src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx`, `src/integrations/supabase/runtime/supabaseRuntime.ts`), but migration shims and global instrumentation weaken ownership boundaries.
Home and gallery areas are decomposed into feature folders, yet some public APIs retain transitional aliases and compatibility exports.
Boundary mapping is explicit in generation mappers (`src/domains/generation/mappers/generationParamsMapper.ts`) with typed conversions, which is strong.

Integration seams are less predictable where global side effects are used for healing/debug (`src/integrations/supabase/instrumentation/*`, `ReconnectScheduler`), coupling error handling, runtime wiring, and diagnostics.
Many internals are direct and readable (`src/app/hooks/useAppExternalDrop.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/fetchWithTimeout.ts`, `src/app/hooks/useSplitViewScroll.ts`).

Some files contain avoidable noise and long inline commentary that reduces signal density (`src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/reconnect/ReconnectScheduler.ts`).
`src/features/ai/hooks/useAIInteractionService.ts` repeats near-identical operation shells instead of a smaller policy-driven helper, fragmenting one concern into many thin wrappers.
`src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts` mixes page orchestration, mutation choreography, pagination, fallback behavior, and compatibility export naming in one large module.

findings.summary: Compatibility shims remain active across Supabase and feature entrypoints, extending old contracts and delaying migration completion.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports deprecated `supabase` proxy with removal target `2026-06-30`.
`src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx` is a migration-compat re-export from shared.
`src/domains/generation/hooks/useSmartPolling.ts` is only a pass-through re-export.
`src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts` keeps preferred name via alias over legacy implementation name.
findings.suggestion: Execute a staged deprecation burn-down: Stage 1 target folders: move remaining call sites to `getSupabaseClient()` and canonical feature/domain modules (`src/features/prompt-editor/*`, `src/domains/generation/hooks/*`). Stage 2 move order: replace `supabase` imports first, then replace re-export entrypoints, then remove alias exports. Stage 3 validation commands: `rg -n "import\s+\{\s*supabase\s*\}" src`, `rg -n "BulkEditControls" src`, `rg -n "useGenerationsPageLogic|useSmartPolling" src`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npx vitest --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts`.

findings.summary: Feature hooks expose incompatible error semantics (throwing, swallowing, and boolean fallbacks), making caller behavior unpredictable.
evidence: `useCredits` throws wrapped `Error` strings for DB failures.
`useApiTokens`/`useApiKeys` throw raw Supabase errors in fetch/update paths.
`useAIInteractionService` `*Safe` methods catch and return defaults (`null`, `[]`, fallback text).
`useGalleryPageState` catches mutation failures and returns `false`.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one boundary contract per hook family (for example: throw typed domain errors from internals and expose status/error objects at hook boundary), then add shared normalization helper and migrate hooks in priority order: billing/auth hooks first, AI hooks second, gallery handlers third.

findings.summary: Supabase diagnostics and healing logic are implemented via global monkey patches, creating hidden cross-module coupling.
evidence: `installRealtimeInstrumentation` overrides `console.warn` globally to trigger reconnect behavior.
`installWindowOnlyInstrumentation` rewrites `window.WebSocket`, `window.onerror`, and `window.onunhandledrejection`.
Runtime init installs these hooks centrally, so unrelated modules inherit side effects implicitly.
findings.suggestion: Replace global monkey patches with scoped observers: subscribe to explicit Supabase realtime/auth events, route health signals through a typed event bus, and keep diagnostic probes behind isolated dev-only modules activated by explicit bootstrap flags.

findings.summary: Peripheral root wrappers and in-package pass-through modules reduce navigability and blur ownership boundaries.
evidence: `holistic_context.structure.root_files` marks root `tailwind/vite/vitest` entries as peripheral with fan_in < 5 while real config lives in `config/*`.
`BulkEditControls.tsx` (feature) and `useSmartPolling.ts` (domain) mostly point into `src/shared/*`, indicating unresolved import-affinity ownership.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: keep all build/test config entrypoints under `config/` and establish canonical feature/domain implementations under `src/features/prompt-editor/components/` and `src/domains/generation/hooks/`. Stage 2 move order: (1) update importers to canonical in-package modules, (2) convert wrappers to deprecated aliases with TODO owners, (3) remove wrappers after zero-usage check. Stage 3 import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "from '@/shared/components/BulkEditControls'|from '@/shared/hooks/useSmartPolling'" src`, `rg -n "vite\.config|vitest\.config|tailwind\.config" -g"*.{ts,js,json}"`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npx vitest --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts`, `npx vitest --config config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts`.
